generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR sensor for 3D environment mapping with 10Hz update rate and 10-meter range for obstacle detection in autonomous vehicle scenarios."
    DTDFVocab:producedData HokuyoLiDARPointCloudData, HokuyoLiDARObjectTracks
]

instance IntelRealSenseD435iCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Intel RealSense D435i depth camera for spatial perception and 3D visualization in autonomous vehicle systems."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CameraDepthData, CameraVisualData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Onboard sensor system collecting ego vehicle speed, steering angle, and orientation data for real-time adaptive cruise control."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleSpeedData, SteeringAngleData, VehicleOrientationData
]

instance MotorActuationSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor system monitoring motor actuation parameters including torque, RPM, and voltage for closed-loop control in autonomous vehicles."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorTorqueData, MotorRpmData, MotorVoltageData
]

instance SteeringServoSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor system tracking steering servo parameters including angle and position for precise vehicle maneuvering."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringAngleData, SteeringPositionData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance HokuyoLiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw point cloud data from Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR transmitted via Wi-Fi UDP at high frequency (10Hz) for real-time processing."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR
]

instance HokuyoLiDARObjectTracks : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Processed object tracks derived from Hokuyo LiDAR point cloud clustering for obstacle detection and tracking."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR
]

instance CameraDepthData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Depth data from Intel RealSense D435i camera for spatial perception and 3D visualization preprocessing."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelRealSenseD435iCamera
]

instance CameraVisualData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Visual data from Intel RealSense D435i camera for 3D visualization and environmental awareness."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelRealSenseD435iCamera
]

instance VehicleSpeedData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Ego vehicle speed data collected at high frequency for real-time adaptive cruise control calculations."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance SteeringAngleData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Steering angle data transmitted for vehicle dynamics modeling and control adjustments."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor, SteeringServoSensor
]

instance VehicleOrientationData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Vehicle orientation data including yaw, pitch, and roll for navigation and path planning."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance MotorTorqueData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Motor torque data for closed-loop control and actuation validation."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorActuationSensor
]

instance MotorRpmData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Motor RPM data for performance monitoring and control adjustments."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorActuationSensor
]

instance MotorVoltageData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Motor voltage data for actuation command validation and energy management."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorActuationSensor
]

instance SteeringPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Steering servo position data for precise vehicle maneuvering and control feedback."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringServoSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance PointCloudClusteringModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model converting raw LiDAR point cloud data into structured object tracks using clustering algorithms for obstacle detection."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2PointCloudProcessingEnabler
]

instance VehicleDynamicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based model representing vehicle dynamics including speed, steering, and orientation for predictive control."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulinkControlEnabler
]

instance AdaptiveCruiseControlModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Control model computing desired speed based on lead vehicle tracking and clustering algorithms for ACC systems."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2ControlEnabler
]

instance LiDAROcclusionDetectionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "AI/ML model deployed on NVIDIA Jetson for detecting LiDAR occlusions and sensor anomalies in real-time."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo JetsonAIDetectionEnabler
]

instance HokuyoLiDARPointCloudDataProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured point cloud data after preprocessing including noise reduction and filtering for visualization."
    DTDFVocab:fromData HokuyoLiDARPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2PointCloudProcessingEnabler, Unity3DVisualizationEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated vehicle state data including speed, orientation, and steering parameters for control algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleSpeedData, VehicleOrientationData, SteeringAngleData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulinkControlEnabler, ROS2ControlEnabler
]

instance ObjectTrackData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed object tracks from LiDAR point cloud clustering for obstacle avoidance and path planning."
    DTDFVocab:fromData HokuyoLiDARObjectTracks
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2ControlEnabler, Unity3DVisualizationEnabler
]

instance MotorActuationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed motor actuation data including torque, RPM, and voltage for closed-loop control validation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorTorqueData, MotorRpmData, MotorVoltageData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulinkControlEnabler
]

instance SteeringActuationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed steering servo data including angle and position for precise vehicle maneuvering."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SteeringAngleData, SteeringPositionData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2ControlEnabler
]

instance VisualizationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Enhanced visual data for 3D visualization including filtered point clouds and object tracks."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CameraDepthData, CameraVisualData, HokuyoLiDARPointCloudDataProcessed
    DTDFVocab:inputTo Unity3DVisualizationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ROS2PointCloudProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2-based enabler for point cloud clustering and tracking, handling raw LiDAR data conversion to structured object tracks."
    DTDFVocab:enables PointCloudProcessingService
]

instance MATLABSimulinkControlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink platform for control system design including PID controllers and state-space models for vehicle dynamics."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlAlgorithmService, ModelPredictiveControlService
]

instance ROS2ControlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2 middleware for real-time control communication between sensor data, actuation commands, and ACC algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:enables AdaptiveCruiseControlService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance JetsonAIDetectionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "NVIDIA Jetson NX platform with CUDA-accelerated models for real-time anomaly detection including LiDAR occlusion."
    DTDFVocab:enables AnomalyDetectionService
]

instance Unity3DVisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Unity-based 3D visualization enabler for immersive monitoring of vehicle state, object tracks, and environmental data."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

instance OPCUACommunicationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "OPC UA protocol enabler for secure, platform-independent data exchange between physical and digital twin components."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAcquisitionService
]

instance MQTTCommunicationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol enabler for lightweight IoT messaging of sensor telemetry and control commands."
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementService
]

// C6: Services
instance PointCloudProcessingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service converting raw LiDAR point cloud data into structured object tracks for obstacle detection and tracking."
    DTDFVocab:provides LiDARObjectTrackingInsight
]

instance ControlAlgorithmService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service implementing PID controllers and state-space models for vehicle dynamics control based on sensor inputs."
    DTDFVocab:provides VehicleControlOptimizationInsight
]

instance AdaptiveCruiseControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service computing desired speed based on lead vehicle tracking and clustering algorithms for ACC systems."
    DTDFVocab:provides DesiredSpeedRecommendationAction, EmergencyBrakingCommandAction
]

instance AnomalyDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service detecting LiDAR occlusions and sensor anomalies using AI/ML models deployed on NVIDIA Jetson."
    DTDFVocab:provides LiDAROcclusionAlertInsight
]

instance ModelPredictiveControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service implementing model predictive control for trajectory optimization and predictive maintenance."
    DTDFVocab:provides TrajectoryOptimizationInsight
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing 3D visualization of vehicle state, object tracks, and environmental data for operational oversight."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTime3DVisualizationInsight
]

instance DataAcquisitionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service collecting real-time sensor data including LiDAR scans, IMU readings, and environmental variables via OPC UA."
    DTDFVocab:provides SensorDataMonitoringInsight
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling dynamic adjustment of ACC parameters and remote control commands via MQTT and ROS2."
    DTDFVocab:provides RemoteACCControlAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance LiDARObjectTrackingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structured object tracks derived from LiDAR point cloud clustering for obstacle detection and avoidance."
]

    base:desc "Optimized control parameters for vehicle dynamics including PID tuning and state-space model adjustments."
]

    base:desc "Automatically computed desired speed based on lead vehicle tracking and clustering algorithms."

]

instance EmergencyBrakingCommandAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic emergency braking command triggered by obstacle detection or system anomalies."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

    base:desc "Alert indicating detected LiDAR occlusions or sensor anomalies requiring manual intervention."
]

instance TrajectoryOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimized vehicle trajectory based on predictive models for efficient path planning and obstacle avoidance."
]

    base:desc "Immersive 3D visualization of vehicle state, object tracks, and environmental data for operational oversight."
]

    base:desc "Real-time monitoring of sensor data including LiDAR scans, IMU readings, and environmental variables."
]

    base:desc "Remote adjustment of ACC parameters including speed thresholds and braking profiles."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance VedderElectronicSpeedController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Electronic speed controller converting desired speed inputs into motor voltage commands for vehicle mobility control."
]

instance TraxxasSlash4x4Chassis : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Vehicle chassis providing mechanical structure and mobility platform for autonomous navigation and control testing."
]

instance MotorActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Electric motor actuator receiving torque and RPM commands for vehicle propulsion and dynamic movement."
]

    base:desc "Steering servo actuator converting angle commands into precise vehicle maneuvering and orientation control."
]

// C1: Environment
instance AutonomousVehicleEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Cyber-biophysical environment for 1/10-scale autonomous vehicle with ACC system operating in dynamic traffic scenarios."
    base:contains AutonomousVehiclePhysicalTwin, AutonomousVehicleDigitalTwin, AutonomousVehicleUserInterface
]

instance AutonomousVehiclePhysicalTwin : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Physical twin system including Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR, Intel RealSense D435i camera, and actuation components for real-time autonomous navigation."
    base:contains HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR, IntelRealSenseD435iCamera, VehicleStateSensor, MotorActuationSensor, SteeringServoSensor,
                VedderElectronicSpeedController, TraxxasSlash4x4Chassis, MotorActuator, SteeringServoActuator
]

instance AutonomousVehicleDigitalTwin : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Digital twin entity providing computational models and data representation of the physical autonomous vehicle for real-time control and monitoring."
    base:contains PointCloudClusteringModel, VehicleDynamicsModel, AdaptiveCruiseControlModel, LiDAROcclusionDetectionModel,
                HokuyoLiDARPointCloudDataProcessed, VehicleStateData, ObjectTrackData, MotorActuationData, SteeringActuationData,
                VisualizationData
]

instance AutonomousVehicleUserInterface : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "User interface entity enabling real-time data monitoring, report generation, and remote management of ACC systems."
    base:contains VisualizationService, RemoteManagementService, DataAcquisitionService
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **Virtual-to-Physical Interaction** in this DTS is limited to the following control flows:
  1. **Command Types**:
     - **Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) Commands**: Generated by the *Controlling FE* component, which processes digital twin data (e.g., object tracks, projected path) to issue throttle/brake adjustments.
     - **Actuation Commands**: Directed to motor and servo systems (e.g., *Actuation FE* for motor control), though **steering servo actuation commands are not explicitly described**—only steering angle data collection/monitoring is mentioned.
  2. **Control Message Formats**:
     - Commands are serialized and translated via the *Data Translation* component to align with physical system message types (e.g., CAN bus or proprietary actuator protocols). No specific format (e.g., ROS2 messages, binary frames) is detailed.
  3. **Validation & Authentication**:
     - **Not in Document**: No mechanisms (e.g., digital signatures, checksums) for validating command integrity or authenticating sources are described.
  4. **Feedback Systems**:
     - **Not in Document**: No explicit feedback loops (e.g., acknowledgment signals, error codes) from physical actuators to the digital twin are mentioned.
  5. **Conflict Resolution**:
     - **Not in Document**: No arbitration strategies (e.g., priority-based command queuing, deadlock prevention) are specified for concurrent control requests.
  6. **Command Execution Confirmation**:
     - **Not in Document**: No confirmation protocols (e.g., handshake signals, status flags) are described to verify actuator execution."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System (DTS) enforces a **100 ms time synchronization accuracy** between physical and digital components to enable computation offloading and coordination, adhering to hardware limitations. This temporal granularity is critical for safety-critical vehicular applications, where stricter clock synchronization (e.g., sub-100 ms) is mandated by many standards. The system does not explicitly specify distinct time scales for different services but implies real-time constraints for sensor-actuator loops, data pre-processing, and device communication within the **Physical Twin Entity**. Latency requirements are implicitly tied to the **ROS2-based execution model**, where each Functionality Element (FE) is implemented as an individual ROS2 package, ensuring deterministic behavior for time-sensitive operations like **Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking** and actuation commands. No explicit mention of dynamic time scaling or adaptive synchronization mechanisms is provided."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DTS follows a **modular, entity-based orchestration pattern** grounded in **ISO 23247 RA**, with deviations to accommodate automotive-specific requirements. Key components are organized into three **hardware-deployed entities**:
  1. **Physical Twin Entity**: Hosts sensor/actuator interfaces, data pre-processing, and general automotive functions (e.g., *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*), diverging from ISO 23247’s *OME* by centralizing resource-agnostic logic.
  2. **Digital Twin Entity**: Manages data storage, control mechanisms, and device communication, aligning with ISO 23247’s data-centric functions.
  3. **User Entity**: Contains the *User Interface FE*, split into *3D Visualization* and *Control Panel* subcomponents for user-facing operations.
  **Resource management** is implicit via ROS2’s package-based deployment, where each FE is an isolated ROS2 node. **System-wide coordination** relies on ROS2’s **topic-based publish-subscribe model** and **service calls**, ensuring inter-entity communication (e.g., between *Physical Twin* sensors and *Digital Twin* control logic). The architecture lacks explicit orchestration frameworks (e.g., Kubernetes, YARP) but leverages ROS2’s **distributed computing** capabilities for real-time synchronization (e.g., 100 ms clock accuracy)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The ISO 23247 standard (Part 2) does not provide explicit guidelines on **model fidelity requirements**, **validation/verification methods**, or **uncertainty quantification** for Digital Twin (DT) systems. The architecture defines **entities** (e.g., Digital Twin Entity, Data Collection and Device Control Entity, User Entity) and their functional roles but leaves implementation details—including fidelity trade-offs, data accuracy thresholds, or error correction mechanisms—unaddressed. The **User Entity** is responsible for interfacing with the DT, potentially enabling manual validation or monitoring, but no automated or systematic validation processes (e.g., real-time data comparison, statistical analysis) are specified. The standard emphasizes **entity-based modularity** over prescriptive quality assurance, implying that fidelity and validity may depend on external frameworks or domain-specific practices not detailed in the document."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
  **Technical Network Infrastructure:**
  - **Communication Protocols:**
    - **ROS2 (Robot Operating System 2):** Used for intra-entity (software component) communication within the digital twin system.
    - **Wi-Fi with UDP:** Used for inter-entity communication (e.g., between physical twin and digital twin entities).

  - **Network Topology and Architecture:**
    - **Hierarchical:** Physical sensors (LiDAR, cameras, motor/steering servos) send data to the digital twin via Wi-Fi (UDP).
    - **Modular:** ROS2 network connects software components (e.g., Data Translation, Clustering & Tracking, Visualization) in a decentralized manner.

  - **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements:**
    - **High Bandwidth:** Required for LiDAR point cloud data (high-resolution, frequent updates).
    - **Low Latency:** Critical for real-time monitoring and control (e.g., ACC commands must execute within milliseconds).

  - **Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms:**
    - Not explicitly described. Likely relies on ROS2's fault-tolerant features (e.g., message retries, component recovery) and Wi-Fi redundancy (e.g., failover connections).

  - **Security Protocols for Network Communication:**
    - Not explicitly described. Assumed to include:
      - ROS2 security (e.g., TLS for message encryption, authentication via certificates).
      - Wi-Fi security (e.g., WPA3 encryption, firewall rules).
  "
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin System (DTS) Hosting and Deployment** architecture is designed as a **hybrid edge-cloud system**, with critical components distributed between the **Physical Twin Entity (vehicle)** and **Digital Twin Entity (DT)**. Key deployment details include:
- **Hosting Platforms**:
  - **Edge Deployment (Physical Twin Entity)**:
    - **Hardware**: The vehicle’s autonomy components include an **NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano microcomputer**, responsible for real-time sensor data processing (e.g., LiDAR clustering/tracking, vehicle state aggregation) and actuation control.
    - **Software Components**:
      - **Data Collecting FE**: Gathers raw sensor data (LiDAR, motor, steering, servo, vehicle state).
      - **Data Pre-Processing FE**: Processes raw data (e.g., point cloud clustering/tracking) before transmission.
      - **Actuation FE**: Executes commands (e.g., motor voltage adjustments) based on DT-generated control signals.
      - **Controlling FE (ACC)**: Implements core ACC logic, though the document specifies that **only the ACC system is offloaded from the vehicle to the DT**, implying the Physical Twin Entity retains basic autonomy functions.
  - **Digital Twin Entity (DT)**:
    - **Hosting**: Deployed on an external platform (not specified in the document, but implied to be **cloud or on-premise servers** for scalability and storage).
    - **Software Components**:
      - **Data Translation FE**: Serializes/deserializes data between entities, performs unit conversions, and aligns message types.
      - **Controlling FE (ACC)**: Hosts the primary ACC logic, including path planning and obstacle avoidance, leveraging the DT’s computational resources.
      - **Presentation FE**: Manages **3D Visualization** and **User Interface FE** for real-time monitoring and control.
      - **Reporting FE**: Generates reports and logs for post-processing analysis.
- **Computational and Storage Requirements**:
  - **Edge (Vehicle)**:
    - **Computational**: The Jetson Orin Nano handles real-time sensor fusion, clustering, and low-level actuation, with constraints on power and thermal management.
    - **Storage**: Local storage for temporary sensor buffers and pre-processed data; no explicit details on capacity.
  - **DT (Cloud/On-Premise)**:
    - **Computational**: High-performance computing for ACC logic, path planning, and visualization rendering (e.g., 3D models of the vehicle and environment).
    - **Storage**: External data storage for historical logs, reports, and large-scale datasets (e.g., LiDAR point clouds, driving trajectories).
- **Scalability Mechanisms**:
  - **Not in Document**: The document does not specify how the system scales horizontally (e.g., adding more DT instances) or vertically (e.g., upgrading hardware). The offloading of ACC to the DT suggests a **modular design**, but no explicit scalability protocols are described.
- **Deployment Models Supported**:
  - **Hybrid Edge-Cloud**: Critical real-time functions (e.g., sensor processing, actuation) run on the edge (vehicle), while computationally intensive tasks (e.g., ACC logic, visualization) are offloaded to the DT.
  - **Not in Document**: No mention of containerization (e.g., Docker), orchestration (e.g., Kubernetes), or multi-cloud deployment strategies.
- **Availability and Reliability Assurance**:
  - **Not in Document**: No details on high-availability configurations (e.g., redundant DT instances, load balancing) or service-level agreements (SLAs) for uptime. The system’s reliability depends on the underlying network (Wi-Fi/UDP) and edge hardware (Jetson Orin Nano).
"
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system adheres to the **ISO 23247 standard**, specifically **Part 2: Reference Architecture**, which defines an **entity-based framework** for manufacturing DTs. Key compliance aspects include:
  - **Entity-based Reference Architecture**: Mandates decomposition into **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)**, **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**, **Digital Twin Entity**, and **User Entity**, each with defined responsibilities.
  - **Interoperability Standards**: Implicitly supports **OPC Unified Architecture** (UA) for communication between entities, ensuring seamless data exchange and device control.
  - **Information Exchange**: Part 4 of the standard (not detailed here) likely addresses **data formats, protocols, and semantic interoperability** for cross-system compatibility.
  - **Certification Requirements**: Not explicitly mentioned, but adherence to ISO 23247 implies compliance with **manufacturing industry best practices** and **digital twin governance frameworks**.
  The standard does not specify **certification bodies** or **compliance frameworks** beyond ISO, but it aligns with broader **Industry 4.0** and **smart manufacturing** initiatives. **Interoperability** is a core focus, ensuring the DT system can integrate with other **IIoT (Industrial Internet of Things) platforms** or **enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems** via standardized interfaces."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System (DTS) incorporates **safety-critical system considerations** by implementing **Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC)** as specified in **ISO 15622:2018**, which defines performance requirements and test procedures for such systems. The system adheres to **real-time communication constraints**, ensuring **network latency of at most 100 milliseconds** for V2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) services, as referenced in **3GPP TS 22.185 (Release 15, 2018)**. While the documents do not explicitly mention **cybersecurity protocols, access control mechanisms, or authentication systems**, the **safety-critical nature** of the system implies compliance with **fail-safe mechanisms** and **risk mitigation strategies** inherent to ACC implementations. No direct references to **ISO 26262-1:2018** (functional safety for road vehicles) are provided in the source text."
]
